finding_results.notes: Varied the repeated fetal-stress template into distinct bodily registers (pelvic drag, spinal cramp with dizziness, lower-belly/back stitch) while retaining one high-rib breath-snag beat for continuity pressure. Pattern repetition is reduced below the fail threshold.
finding_results.summary: Bodily-risk beats now vary in syntax and sensation source instead of repeating the same movement-plus-breath-loss construction.

finding_results.notes: Reframed repeated shared-wall interruption scaffolding with varied trigger mechanics: tactile partition shudder, electrical dip/object-state shift, lane-level sound image, and localized next-door pantry action. Repeated through-wall phrasing no longer drives the targeted turning points.
finding_results.summary: Turning-point interruptions now use varied sensory triggers and sentence shapes, reducing mechanical shared-wall repetition.

summary: Implemented focused prose/copyedit revisions for chapter 6: reduced formulaic bodily-stress repetition, diversified interruption mechanics, preserved continuity and heading contract, and maintained character-true pressure language without sanitization.
